articles.title: Cotton Falling Lower on Thursday
articles.expand: Cotton prices are down 95 to 137 points so far. Futures saw modest weakness on a Wednesday correction, with contracts 6 to 73 points lower. The outside markets were generally stronger, as Crude oil was up $4.32 following the US striking Iran overnight and again today, as President Trump stated...

articles.title: Dollar Slips as Stocks Strengthen
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) is down by -0.11% today. The dollar is moving lower today as strength in stocks has reduced liquidity demand for the currency. The dollar dropped to its low after US Jun existing home sales unexpectedly declined. Losses in the dollar are limited after US weekly...

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar jumps to a three-week high after hawkish RBNZ rate hike
articles.expand: NZD/USD jumps 1% on Thursday, extending gains for a second consecutive day after the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) delivered a hawkish rate hike on Wednesday, while a softer US Dollar (USD) provides additional support.

articles.title: Japanese Yen rises as US jobless claims fail to support US Dollar
articles.expand: USD/JPY trades lower near the 162.30 area on Thursday, retreating from recent highs as the Japanese Yen (JPY) recovers some ground. The US Dollar (USD) fails to receive support from stronger-than-expected United States (US) labor market data.

articles.title: United States Dollar Index falls as Middle East calm cools inflation fears
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which tracks the buck’s value against a basket of six currencies, is down 0.14% to 100.93 as tensions in the Middle East ease, driving Oil prices lower and trimming Fed hawkish bets spurred by the energy shock.

articles.title: Australian Dollar rises as US Dollar softens despite geopolitical tensions
articles.expand: AUD/USD trades around 0.6940 on Thursday at the time of writing, up 0.16% on the day, as the weaker US Dollar (USD) supports the pair despite persistent tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: Sentiment improves as risks fade against US Dollar – Scotiabank
articles.expand: Scotiabank strategists Shaun Osborne and Eric Theoret note the Canadian Dollar (CAD) is flat on Thursday but modestly firmer versus the US Dollar (USD) this week, making it a mild outperformer.

articles.title: British Pound elevates despite firm US claims
articles.expand: GBP/USD trades higher near the 1.3400 area on Thursday, as the US Dollar (USD) fails to find support from stronger-than-expected United States (US) jobless claims data and hawkish signals in the latest Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) Minutes.

articles.title: European Central Bank: September hike prospects stay firm – Nordea
articles.expand: Nordea’s Jan von Gerich argues that the European Central Bank (ECB) is likely to continue tightening policy, with the outlook heavily dependent on Middle East developments and energy prices.

articles.title: Gold firms but lacks momentum amid US-Iran tensions, hawkish Fed bets
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) firms on Thursday as a mildly softer US Dollar (USD) and a modest pullback in US Treasury yields help the precious metal recover some ground after three consecutive days of losses.

articles.title: Singapore Dollar: Upside risks building as USD stays firm – DBS
articles.expand: DBS Group Research economist Eugene Leow warns that shorter-term Singapore Dollar (SGD) rates may face upside pressure despite recent flush liquidity. He notes SGD rates have decoupled from USD rates, with spreads stretched, while Fed hike expectations remain sticky and the USD strong.

articles.title: U.S. Weekly Jobless Claims Unexpectedly Edge Down To 215,000
articles.expand: The Labor Department released a report on Thursday unexpectedly showing a modest decrease in first-time claims for U.S. unemployment benefits in the week ended July 4th. The report said initial jobless claims edged down to 215,000, a decrease of 2,000 from the previous week's revised level of 217,000.

articles.title: Euro holds firm as traders assess geopolitical risks, interest rate outlook
articles.expand: EUR/USD holds modest gains on Thursday, supported by some softness in the US Dollar (USD) as markets assess renewed hostilities in the Middle East. At the time of writing, the pair trades around 1.1444, up 0.25% on the day.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar remains rangebound as weaker US Dollar offsets lower Oil prices
articles.expand: USD/CAD trades around 1.4170 on Thursday at the time of writing, virtually unchanged on the day, as weakness in the US Dollar (USD) offsets the negative impact of lower Oil prices on the Canadian Dollar (CAD).

articles.title: NZD Leads on Growth Optimism While CAD Awaits Jobs Test
articles.expand: New Zealand Dollar outperformed across the board today after a much stronger-than-expected manufacturing survey reinforced confidence that the economy is gaining momentum following the Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s rate hike. At the other end of the spectrum, Canadian Dollar is the weakest major currency despite another sharp rebound in oil prices, underscoring that structural […] The post NZD Leads on Growth Optimism While CAD Awaits Jobs Test appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Fed: Hawkish minutes keep upside rate risks – ING
articles.expand: ING strategists Michiel Tukker and Benjamin Schroeder note that the Federal Reserve’s June minutes confirmed a more hawkish stance despite an unchanged policy rate.

articles.title: US Dollar: Fed minutes flag supply-driven inflation risks – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists highlight that the June Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) Minutes showed rising concern over inflation risks, even as the United States (US) labor market remains stable.

articles.title: Japanese Yen gains modestly, remains near multi-decade lows
articles.expand: USD/JPY trades slightly lower on Thursday as a mildly softer US Dollar (USD) lends support to the Japanese Yen (JPY). At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 162.45, hovering near 40-year highs.

articles.title: ECB Minutes: Inflation Damage Already Too Broad to Ignore
articles.expand: The minutes of the ECB’s June meeting reinforced the Governing Council’s conviction that inflationary pressures had become too broad and persistent to justify waiting any longer before tightening policy. While all members unanimously backed a 25 basis point rate hike, the discussion showed policymakers had fundamentally reassessed the nature of the Middle East energy shock. articles.title: Confirmed: ECB Accounts reveal growing concerns over upside inflation risks
articles.expand: The European Central Bank (ECB) released the accounts of its latest monetary policy meeting on Thursday, revealing growing concern among policymakers over persistent inflationary risks.

articles.title: Fed: Divergent inflation paths shape rate outlook – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale’s Jan Groen notes that the June Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) minutes confirmed a hawkish hold, with the Summary of Economic Projections (SEP) showing an even split between members favoring unchanged or lower rates and those preferring hikes.

articles.title: GBP/CAD Hits Decade High as USMCA Shock Adds New Driver Ahead of Jobs Data
articles.expand: GBP/CAD climbed to its highest level in a decade this week, reflecting an increasingly powerful divergence between a Pound supported by fading domestic political risks and a Canadian Dollar facing mounting structural headwinds. Sterling continues to benefit from the unwinding of sizeable speculative short positions built ahead of Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s resignation, while Bank […] The post GBP/CAD Hits Decade High as USMCA Shock Adds New Driver Ahead of Jobs Data appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Japan may be changing its Yen strategy, but markets don’t look scared
articles.expand: Japan may be changing its intervention playbook, but that might not be enough to rescue the battered Yen. With USD/JPY hovering at four-decade highs, the currency’s weakness is being driven less by speculative pressure and more by a powerful structural force: the wide US-Japan rate gap.

articles.title: USD/CAD Analysis for July 9, 2026: Trend Lines Tested on Divided FOMC and Canada Jobs
articles.expand: An oil supply shock this week flipped the usual script for the Canadian dollar, as Trump declared the US-Iran ceasefire over. Can the Canadian employment report turn things around for USD/CAD?

articles.title: Chart Art: USD/CAD Tests Key Range Support Ahead of Canada Jobs Release
articles.expand: USD/CAD is sitting on a major support just as Canada is printing a top-tier economic update! Will bulls defend the floor, or are bears about to get their breakout?

articles.title: TA Alert of the Day: GBP/JPY Keltner Channel Breach Flags Potential Pullback
articles.expand: GBP/JPY’s upper Keltner Channel breach puts focus on whether buyers can sustain momentum or whether price snaps back inside the channel.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Bears now look at the 200-day SMA
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar remains unable to find sustainable upside traction, keeping its trade below the key 0.7000 yardstick for now. So far, the constructive tone should remain unchallenged while above its critical 200-day SMA.

articles.title: 2.50%: Why the Kiwi's first hike in three years is a wager on a number nobody can see
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) raised the Official Cash Rate (OCR) by 25 basis points to 2.50% at 02:00 GMT on Wednesday, its first hike in three years and the moment the bank that cut deeper than any G10 peer last cycle turned to face the other way.

articles.title: RBNZ Raises OCR to 2.50%
articles.expand: The RBNZ raised its Official Cash Rate to 2.50%, its first hike in three years, but won’t commit to a timeline for what comes next.

articles.title: Pound Awaits Tighter Policy from Bank of England
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex GBP/USD declined to 1.3352 on Wednesday amid a general deterioration in the external environment and a decline in risk appetite. The escalation of tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and Iran’s attacks on facilities in Kuwait and Bahrain have prompted investors to move away from riskier assets. Earlier, the pound had […]

articles.title: Oil Tests a Return of War Premium as Markets Await the Fed’s Hidden Debate
articles.expand: Markets were driven by two competing sources of uncertainty today as Brent crude surged toward the $80 mark while investors awaited the release of the June FOMC minutes for the first real insight into Chair Kevin Warsh’s policy thinking. Dollar traded firmer alongside oil, while Kiwi outperformed following the Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s rate […] The post Oil Tests a Return of War Premium as Markets Await the Fed’s Hidden Debate appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Australian Dollar After RBA's Hunter Hawkish Remarks
articles.expand: The Australian dollar strengthened against other major currencies in the Asian session on Wednesday, as the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) kept the door open for additional monetary policy tightening, if necessary, to bring inflation back to the central bank's target.

articles.title: NZD/USD Analysis for July 8, 2026: NZD Faces Dollar Test After RBNZ Stands Pat
articles.expand: NZD/USD faces a fresh test as RBNZ caution meets Fed uncertainty with key chart levels keeping traders on their toes.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Rises Against Majors
articles.expand: The Australian dollar strengthened against other major currencies in the Asian session on Wednesday.

articles.title: New Zealand Central Bank Hikes Rate For First Time Since 2023
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand raised its benchmark interest rate for the first time in more than three years to bring inflation to target and hinted at further tightening. The Monetary Policy Committee, governed by Anna Breman, decided to lift the Official Cash Rate by 25 basis points to 2.50 percent. This was the first hike since May 2023.

articles.title: NZD/USD Recovers After RBNZ, but Higher Bar for Next Hike Caps Rally
articles.expand: The New Zealand dollar received the boost that normally accompanies a rate hike, but the rally quickly lost momentum. The Reserve Bank of New Zealand delivered a widely anticipated 25 basis point increase in the Official Cash Rate to 2.50%, yet investors stopped short of pricing a more aggressive tightening cycle. Instead, the market came […] The post NZD/USD Recovers After RBNZ, but Higher Bar for Next Hike Caps Rally appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: RBNZ Raises OCR, Consensus Masks Diverging Inflation Views
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand raised the Official Cash Rate by 25 basis points to 2.50%, with the Monetary Policy Committee reaching the decision by consensus despite differing assessments of the inflation outlook. Policymakers agreed that a further withdrawal of monetary stimulus was warranted to return inflation to the 2% midpoint, even though the […] The post RBNZ Raises OCR, Consensus Masks Diverging Inflation Views appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: RBA’s Hunter: Supply Shocks Don’t Mean Inflation Can Be Ignored
articles.expand: Reserve Bank of Australia Assistant Governor Sarah Hunter said central banks cannot automatically dismiss inflation caused by supply shocks, arguing that persistent disruptions may still require a monetary policy response. “While supply shocks create difficult trade-offs, they do not lessen the importance of maintaining low and stable inflation,” she said, emphasizing that the RBA remains […] The post RBA’s Hunter: Supply Shocks Don’t Mean Inflation Can Be Ignored appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: BoJ’s Asada: Demand-Driven Inflation Needed Before Another Rate Hike
articles.expand: Bank of Japan board member Toichiro Asada said he would only support another interest rate hike once inflation is clearly being driven by stronger domestic demand rather than temporary cost pressures. Speaking after dissenting against the BoJ’s June rate increase, Asada said, “I would support another rate hike only after confirming demand-driven inflation,” signaling that […] The post BoJ’s Asada: Demand-Driven Inflation Needed Before Another Rate Hike appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Speech: Understanding Supply Shocks and Their Implications for Monetary Policy
articles.expand: Speech by Sarah Hunter, Assistant Governor (Economic), to the Australian Conference of Economists 2026

articles.title: Yen Still Under Pressure: Markets Await Action from Authorities
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex USD/JPY is holding near 161.84 on Tuesday, with the yen close to 40-year lows. Pressure on the Japanese currency remains as market participants continue to bet against it, with no visible currency interventions from Japanese authorities having materialised. At the same time, investors are closely monitoring potential action from Tokyo. Japanese […]

articles.title: The Next Dollar Catalyst Is CPI. The Bigger Story Is Inside the Fed.
articles.expand: Financial markets spent much of today’s session waiting rather than reacting. Most major currency pairs traded comfortably within yesterday’s ranges as investors resisted making large directional bets ahead of tomorrow’s RBNZ decision and next week’s US inflation report. Dollar and Yen were the day’s strongest performers, but neither attracted enough follow-through buying to establish fresh […] The post The Next Dollar Catalyst Is CPI. The Bigger Story Is Inside the Fed. appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: ESRB warns of vulnerabilities in the financial system linked to frontier AI models
articles.expand: The European Systemic Risk Board (ESRB), the EU’s macroprudential oversight authority, is issuing a formal warning regarding vulnerabilities in the financial system linked to frontier AI models. The ESRB's warning calls for relevant authorities to reflect these risks in supervisory and oversight work.

articles.title: ECB’s Panetta: Fragile Outlook Means No Predetermined Path for Rates
articles.expand: European Central Bank Governing Council member Fabio Panetta said the Eurozone economy continues to face a delicate balance between persistent inflation risks and weakening growth, arguing that policymakers should avoid committing to any predetermined path for interest rates. “The outlook remains fragile. Upside risks to inflation continue to coexist with downside risks to growth,” he […] The post ECB’s Panetta: Fragile Outlook Means No Predetermined Path for Rates appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: GBP/CHF Nears Trend-Reversal Zone as Political Risk Fades and Sterling Shorts Unwind
articles.expand: Sterling’s rally has been about more than better sentiment toward the UK. It has been driven by the disappearance of one of the market’s biggest bearish trades. As political uncertainty faded following the resolution of Labour’s leadership transition, investors who had built sizeable short Sterling positions found themselves on the wrong side of the market. articles.expand: European Central Bank Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel cautioned that the recent decline in oil prices should not be interpreted as a return to pre-war inflation conditions, arguing that underlying price pressures remain elevated. “Does the decline in oil prices mean that we are back to the pre-war situation? articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Has the recovery kicked in?
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar remains in a consolidative phase below the key 0.7000 threshold for now, while the constructive tone remains unchanged above the critical 200-day SMA.

articles.title: 2 Reasons to Sell the Australia Dollar Here
articles.expand: September Australian dollar futures present a selling opportunity on more price weakness.

articles.title: USD/JPY Bulls Return as Japan’s Intervention Window Closes
articles.expand: USD/JPY bulls are back. After last week’s intervention scare briefly knocked the pair lower, Yen selling returned today as the most obvious window for Japanese action passed without official intervention. Dollar’s rebound also helped, with markets now appearing to have fully absorbed the dovish surprise from June non-farm payrolls. articles.title: Eurozone Producer Inflation Holds Firm as Core Factory Prices Continue to Rise
articles.expand: Eurozone PPI rose 0.2% mom in May, matching market expectations, while annual producer inflation accelerated from 5.0% yoy to 5.9% yoy, slightly above the 5.7% yoy consensus. The data suggest producer price pressures remained elevated even as monthly price growth moderated from April’s 0.7% increase. The monthly increase reflected diverging trends across sectors. Prices for […] The post Eurozone Producer Inflation Holds Firm as Core Factory Prices Continue to Rise appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Eurozone Retail Sales Rise 0.2% mom in May as Food Spending Leads Growth
articles.expand: Eurozone retail sales rose 0.2% mom in May, matching market expectations and pointing to continued resilience in consumer spending despite a mixed performance across retail categories. Across the broader EU, retail sales increased a stronger 0.5% mom, suggesting consumer demand remained relatively firm in several member states. The monthly increase in the Eurozone was driven […] The post Eurozone Retail Sales Rise 0.2% mom in May as Food Spending Leads Growth appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: The US Dollar just beat the Swiss Franc at its own safe-haven game
articles.expand: As the king among safe havens, the Swiss Franc (CHF) is supposed to benefit from geopolitical shocks such as the Iran war. This time, it didn’t.
